tactic: Mechanism reveal -> first-person stats -> recursion punch
primary_text:
324 Australian companies have raised on Birchal. Most Australians have never invested in one.

Crowd-Sourced Funding lets retail investors own a piece of private Australian companies. Since 2018, 137,000 of them have invested over $234M through us. Naked Life. Our Cow. Medigrowth.

Now we're opening our own raise.

Save your spot. We will tell you the moment the offer goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: How Australian startups actually get funded
description: Save your spot to hear when our CSF offer opens.
_v2_changes: Subject discipline: dropped 'Birchal built the rails' / 'Birchal is opening its own raise' two-Birchal pattern. Single Birchal mention as platform brand; 'we'/'us'/'our' throughout for the company.
Removed pre-2018 preamble ('owning a piece of a private Australian company meant being a venture capitalist...'). Mechanism reveal is in one sentence now.
Brand grid trimmed to 3 names (Naked Life, Our Cow, Medigrowth) instead of 4.
_drc_playbook: 03 Aspirational Identity Sell (B2C considered, solution-aware, sophistication 3 mechanism)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors, currently using ETFs / Sharesies / Pearler.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'invested', 'tell you the moment'. 'Invested' refers to 137,000 historical investors (factual past tense), not a current call to action.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'we will tell you the moment the offer goes live'. No 'we will send' phrasing.
VERBATIM-CSF-WARNING: byte-exact at end of primary_text.
